title: Trademark Application in Vietnam
metaDescription: Trademark registration is essential to protecting your company's rights while doing business in Vietnam. Here's how we help simplify the process.

hero.title: Trademark Application in Vietnam
hero.subtitle: Constantly changing regulations, protecting your assets, including your intellectual property, can seem daunting for a foreign-owned company in Vietnam. Our experienced bilingual legal team makes trademark licensing and international trademark registration easy and affordable. From day one, we'll provide affordable trademark registration, so you can focus on doing business without any worries.

sections.heading: Required Documents for Trademark Application in Vietnam

cells: According to the form of State Agencies
If trading on stock market, minimum 10 billion VND
Proof of payment of fees and charges to State Agencies

Application fee: 150,000 VND. Application publication fee: 120,000 VND. Searching fee for content examination: 180,000 VND/group of products and services. Searching fee for the 7th+ product or service: 30,000 VND/product or service
In case of applying through a representative
Documents certifying the permission to use special signs
In case the trademark required to protect contains symbols, flags, badges of domestic and international agencies or organizations, etc.
Documents proving the right to register
In case such right is acquired by the applicant from another party
If the application has the requirement for priority right

sections.heading: Trademark Application Procedure in Vietnam
body: Navigating the procedure for trademark registration in Vietnam can be complex, but understanding the timeline and steps involved makes it easier. Here's what to expect once the National Office of Intellectual Property of Vietnam (NOIP) receives your trademark application:
Application publication: Your application will be published within 2 months after NOIP accepts it as valid
Content examination: Not exceeding 9 months from the date of application publication
However, the entire procedure to register a trademark can sometimes extend to 18-24 months due to backlog and other factors.

sections.heading: How to apply
body: Submit Directly: You can send your application directly or by post to NOIP's registered office.
Submit Online: Alternatively, use NOIP's Online Public Service Portal for submission.
Validity: The certificate for your trademark is valid for 10 years from the date of issuance. It can be renewed indefinitely, in 10-year increments.

sections.heading: Frequently Asked Questions on Trademark Licensing in Vietnam

items.q: Why is trademark registration important for foreign investors in Vietnam?
items.a: Trademark registration is essential for protecting your brand against copyright infringements, establishing ownership, and preventing competitors from using your brand name or logo. It also enhances legal defense and helps maintain customer trust.

items.q: What are the different types of trademarks recognized in Vietnam?
items.a: Vietnam recognizes several types of trademarks, including word marks, figurative marks, combined marks, collective marks, and certification marks.

items.q: What advantages does trademark registration provide under Vietnamese law?
items.a: Trademark registration grants exclusive rights to use the trademark nationwide, legal authority to prevent unauthorized use, and the ability to take legal action against infringers. It also serves as a strong proof of ownership in legal contexts.

items.q: What steps are involved in the trademark registration process?
items.a: The process involves filing the application with the National Office of Intellectual Property (NOIP), a form examination, publication of the application, and a content examination before the certificate is granted. The entire procedure can take 18-24 months depending on backlog.

items.q: What is the duration of trademark protection in Vietnam?
items.a: A registered trademark in Vietnam is protected for 10 years, with the option for renewal upon expiration.

items.q: Can trademarks be registered for both local and international protection?
items.a: Yes, businesses can register trademarks for local protection within Vietnam or pursue international protection if they operate or plan to operate beyond Vietnam's borders.

items.q: What are the fees associated with trademark registration in Vietnam?
items.a: Fees vary based on the number of classes and goods/services. The minimum filing fee for a single class starts at approximately US$190.00. Additional fees include examination fees, publication fees, and usage fees.
